puzzle0.title: SIGNAL TUNING
puzzle0.description: Tune the emergency frequency until the hidden transmission locks.

event12.text: If you are a recording, lie to me.

event14.text: Actually don't. I am very tired of being lied to by electrical equipment.

event1.text: I will, but your signal is tearing apart.

event3.text: If you want my name, keep the channel alive long enough for me to say it.

event5.text: And if this is some kind of automated rescue protocol, congratulations. You have terrible timing.

event7.text: Which is still better than no timing.

event5.text: Fine. Verify me. I would verify me too.

event7.text: I have been alone long enough to stop trusting my own echo.

event0.title: EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT
event0.content:
CHANNEL: 7.14 MHz
SIGNAL STATE: DISTORTED
VOICEPRINT: UNKNOWN FEMALE
RECOVERED FRAGMENTS:

"...anyone..."
"...relay is drifting..."
"...not Hale..."

Recommended action: manual signal tuning required.
event0.caption: Text reconstruction of distorted emergency audio from channel 7.14.
event0.message: [EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: The carrier keeps drifting like the relay has a death wish.

event6.text: Which, honestly, would fit the general personality of this station.

event8.text: If you can tune it, do it now.

event1.text: Do not let that channel lead.

event3.text: Tune it if you have to, but keep one hand on the exit.

event5.text: And before you ask, yes, I hear how useless that advice is from inside a terminal.

event7.text: Still. One hand on the exit.

event5.text: I had you for a second. Don't make me go back to static.

event10.text: I forgot how to talk to people without wondering if they were already dead.

event17.text: I was starting to think I'd die talking to static.

event19.text: I spent three days talking to a maintenance drone once.

event23.text: Still a better listener than Patel.

event26.text: That was a joke. I think.

event30.text: And if Ethan Hale is on your other channel, you need to stop listening to him.

event1.text: You are speaking with Ethan Hale, aren't you?

event9.text: The problem with Ethan was never that he sounded dangerous.

event13.text: He sounded like the only adult in the room.

event15.text: And on ECHO, that was usually true.

event18.text: That is what made it worse.

event25.text: I am not saying that because I heard a rumor.

event27.text: I am saying it because I saw what was left of the corridor.

event5.text: I don't want you to believe me just because I sound scared.

event7.text: Scared people can still be wrong.

event9.text: I know how insane I sound. I know he's probably been calm, helpful, maybe even funny.

event13.text: He explains the danger until you forget he might be part of it.

event3.text: He got to you first. That's the whole point.

event12.text: I am not asking you to like me. I am asking you to survive long enough to doubt him.

event14.text: Doubt is annoying. Doubt keeps you alive.

event4.text: Of course that's the first thing he says.

event10.text: Operator, she once bypassed an alarm because it annoyed her.

event19.text: See? This is what he does.

event21.text: He makes you argue about the small thing so you stop looking at the blood on the floor.

event38.text: Operator, this is not a conversation. This is a structural failure with opinions.

event40.text: You don't know what you're doing.

event42.text: I know exactly what happens when everyone lets you speak first.

event4.text: I know that probably felt wrong.

event6.text: He makes silence feel like betrayal.

event8.text: I don't have much time, so I'm going to be blunt.

event5.text: But if he starts steering this conversation, I cut the channel myself.

event7.text: And Ethan, before you correct my terminology, yes, I know it is technically a carrier interruption.

event9.text: I am calling it cutting the channel because it sounds better.

event11.text: I was not going to correct that.

event0.title: SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN
event0.content:
CHANNEL SOURCE: 7.14 MHz
VOICEPRINT MATCH: MAYA CHEN
LIFE SIGNS: ACTIVE
OXYGEN: 18%
LOCATION ESTIMATE: DAMAGED RELAY CONSOLE / LOWER ACCESS SPINE
VISUAL FEED: UNAVAILABLE
STATUS: ALIVE / CRITICAL
event0.caption: Automated survivor verification generated from channel telemetry. No image recovered.
event0.message: [SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event11.text: Oxygen is at eighteen percent, which sounds better than it feels.

event13.text: I started naming equipment yesterday. That's usually a bad sign.

event15.text: The cracked relay console is Malcolm.

event17.text: Malcolm and I are not friends.

event19.text: But Malcolm is keeping me alive, so we're being professional.

event22.text: Which is more than I can say for Hale.

event1.text: You want proof. Good. Keep wanting proof.

event3.text: But understand this: the station is very good at giving you records after everyone who could explain them is gone.

event5.text: You will find logs. You will find timestamps. You will find clean little labels for ugly things.

event7.text: What you will not find is the sound Hale made when the corridor decompressed.

event3.text: Hale, Rourke, Lin, Patel, Arman... and me.

event7.text: Lin labeled everything, including things that were already labels.

event9.text: Patel pretended he hated everyone and fixed their coffee ratios when they weren't looking.

event11.text: Arman sang when he was nervous.

event14.text: He sang a lot near the end.

event16.text: See? That's the part records don't keep.

event18.text: They keep death times. Not people.

event0.title: SURVIVOR STATEMENT: MAYA
event0.content:
TEXT TRANSCRIPT / CHANNEL 7.14

MAYA CHEN:
"There were six assigned crew members on ECHO. After the breach, only one confirmed biological survivor remained: me. The signal using Ethan Hale's identity is not a living crew member. Do not allow it to control station routing."

Transcript confidence: 91%
event0.caption: Text transcript reconstructed from Maya's stabilized emergency channel.

event6.text: That is such a clean phrase, isn't it?

event10.text: Like the station is politely avoiding the word alone.

event13.text: And whatever is pretending to be Hale.

event1.text: TRACE: 7.14 MHz / DRIFTING CARRIER / FEMALE VOICEPRINT PARTIAL

event5.text: Hold it steady when it bites. The drift gets worse right before the lock.

event7.text: I hate that sentence. It sounds like something Hale would say.

event5.text: He corrected everybody. Reports. Logs. Messages. Grammar.

event7.text: We almost spaced him for punctuation violations.

event10.text: The annoying part is that he was right most of the time.

event12.text: He could walk into a room, look at a failing system, and make you feel stupid for not hearing the problem breathing.

event19.text: So when I tell you the thing on your channel is not Ethan Hale...

event21.text: I am not saying it because I wanted him gone.
